label: Federal Science Funding Cuts for Great Lakes

comments.text: If you’re connected with NOAA’s GLERL, or other Great Lakes teams affected by federal cuts + shifts, I’d love to hear from you. I’m a ProPublica reporter in Michigan with a longtime interest in water. Background: www.propublica.org/people/anna-... and www.annaclark.net Signal: annaleighclark.14

Sad, unacceptable, and DANGEROUS.
Massive cuts to NOAA, including the Great Lakes Environmental Research Lab (GLERL) are devastating. Among the critical role it serves to the public (as .bsky.social mentions),
GLERL was also the site of my Spotted Gar experiments for my PhD. 😢

"What has NOAA ever done for me in #GreatLakes?" NOAA employees in the region help communities become better prepared for challenges like severe weather, coastal erosion, harmful algal blooms, and invasive species www.youtube.com/watch?v=1n6y...

The work of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration is critical to those in the Great Lakes region for safety and monitoring weather, with a massive regional economy based around the lakes, including in Ohio. Now the NOAA is the crosshairs of Elon Musk and Donald Trump.
NOAA layoffs endanger Great Lakes, experts say: ‘The drivers behind keeping us safe’

The Trump Administration's proposed budget cuts to NOAA would be devastating to the Great Lakes region. Federal funding supports crucial data collection for weather and marine forecasts, coastal resilience and conservation efforts, www.freep.com/story/news/l...
NOAA doesn't just cover the ocean - they do incredibly important work for the #GreatLakes, too. Defending our waters means defending NOAA.

I worked at NWS Cleveland in the mid '90s when it had become a central hub for Great Lakes forecasting as part of an organized effort by NOAA and the shipping community to improve forecasting for the lakes after the Edmund Fitzgerald. Some great NWS work especially at Cleveland made it a success.
They also dramatically increased safety and regulations of ships on the Great Lakes, but yes a large part is NOAA. There are 50 weather buoys on the Great Lakes now, 30 on Lake Superior alone.

Can we file suit to get NOAA in Ann Arbor back? They monitored the health of The Great Lakes, over 20% of the WORLD's surface fresh water! MI has the most National Forest land in the Midwest. Is there anything we can do to prevent him clear cutting it with this EO? www.snopes.com/fact-check/t...

…A number of my friends and colleagues at #NOAA ‘s Great Lakes Environmental Research Lab were just fired by the new administration. Hard workers and wonderful community members dedicated to protecting the #GreatLakes. I’m furious at this pointless destruction of people’s careers and lives.
Today’s #WorldWaterDay and do you know how we should NOT protect the Great Lakes?💦🌊💧 Defund the EPA and fire hundreds of NOAA employees! We must protect this vital source of fresh water!

NOAA programs play an essential role in protecting the Great Lakes and supporting the constituents who call the Lakes home. Firing staff that run these programs jeopardizes our greatest natural treasures. I joined .senate.gov to demand answers on these terminations and potential impacts.
Thank you .house.gov The Great Lakes need .gov .noaa.gov and NOAA Great Lakes Environmental Research labs to maintain and improve our health 🌊🌊

Another timid Strib headline, but the story is clear: DOGE cuts are worsening an already bad Lake Superior National Forest situation - including firefighting (which the administration has lied about) as we face summer after a dry winter. www.startribune.com/how-will-job...

The work of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration is critical to those in the Great Lakes region for safety and monitoring weather, with a massive regional economy based around the lakes, including in Ohio. Now the NOAA is the crosshairs of Elon Musk and Donald Trump

Did you know: NOAA manages US marine fisheries, including the fisheries in the Great Lakes! So if you like having sustainable local fisheries, thank NOAA scientists (and maybe advocate against the indiscriminate firing policies pushed by power-crazed billionaires). #SundayFishSketch #LakeWhitefish

"But funding can’t protect the #GreatLakes if there’s nobody to direct it." Recent staffing cuts could cost the EPA's Great Lakes National Program Office approximately 200 employees - one-fifth of its workforce. www.wbez.org/2025/02/14/a...

Truly damaging and impact will be lasting The the Great Lakes Environmental Research Lab (GLERL) team is smart, hard working and very humble as they work daily to protect the #GreatLakes Sending a big thank you to the amazing team at GLERL in my home town!!!

NOAA is currently facing significant personnel and budget cuts to its Great Lakes offices and programs. We would like to hear from individuals like you, who may be directly or indirectly impacted by NOAA’s research and work on the Great Lakes. This includes YOU! We need your story!

Great Lakes Psychiatry and Psychotherapy PLLC offers pro bono psychological support for climate scientists fired by DOGE from the Ann Arbor Lab. PM me for details. "NOAA federal firings hit Ann Arbor's Great Lakes Environmental Research Lab" www.michiganpublic.org/environment-...

Since January 20, 2025, the #GreatLakes region has lost 19,450 federal jobs including at NOAA's Great Lakes Environmental Research Laboratory (which lost 20% of its workforce) + US Fish & Wildlife Service, threatening scientific research and ecosystem protection www.nemw.org/trump-admini...

From monitoring the safety of our drinking water to predicting snow and floods - NOAA’s Great Lakes Lab does critical work for Michiganders. Check out our latest article for an in depth look into what’s happening to NOAA and what it could mean for our state 👇 open.substack.com/pub/distills...
We need NOAA! We are in a county that neighbors Lake Erie and a healthy lake is a necessity!

I meant to say “Alongside” not “Among” in above post. While my research at NOAA GLERL was not critical to the public (while many of NOAA’s services are), GLERL graciously hosted a grad student with near-zero funding to do his non-game native fish experiments on-site. GLERL was critical to ME.

On a personal note, I worked at the USGS Great Lakes Science Center for a couple of semesters during my MS. I learned so much there and the people were amazing to work with. I’m gutted by the proposed cuts there.

Cuts to NOAA will hurt WNY. This agency’s public servants are tasked with protecting our Great Lakes and public health. These essential workers should be reinstated immediately, before our environment and community is irreparably harmed.

The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration's work is vitally important for the #Midwest, from predicting weather disasters to protecting the #GreatLakes. ELPC joined ~400 organizations & businesses urging Congress to protect .gov & its vital services. docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1F...

The Great Algal Bloom Lakes: Illegal and unconstitutional DOGE cutbacks have gutted the staff at the Great Lakes Environmental Research Laboratory, part of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration.

the union that represents workers in region 5, which includes multiple midwest states, says they’ve already lost 100 workers to layoffs an resignations. a speaker says they currently have fewer epa workers in the great lakes than under the regan admin.

NOAA layoffs hit Great Lakes Environmental Research Laboratory, which posted this on Instagram: "due to a reduction in staff ... communications services and social media will take an indefinite hiatus." These folks often worked closely with Canadians to study climate impacts on the Lakes.

NOAA's Office of National Marine Sanctuaries serves as the trustee for a network of underwater parks encompassing more than 629,000 square miles of marine and Great Lakes waters.

MN is a Great Lakes State. We need this crucial research to support human and environmental health and not to mention important industries like tourism, fishing, shipping, and more. But to the Trump/Musk administration, gutting NOAA to cut taxes for rich is more important.

A while back, a monitoring station on Lake Michigan wasn’t working. During the summer, it is really useful for sailors/boaters. I found a number for GLERL/NOAA, and called it. Person answered, and explained that the entire public information group was no longer.

Just awful. GLERL is crucial to Great Lakes science, communities, and economies. Some of the best folks I've ever had the fortune to work with. There is no overstating just how important their work is to the region or freshwater as a whole
This is a national disgrace 😞😭 it's an anti-intellectual war on science and technology. GLERL is vital to the health of our Great Lakes.
